blocks.content: A massive pit of unfathomable depth formed where Bardvelve had vanished.
Shirone, who had been watching the darkness, turned to face Nane.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Why are you here?
Nane, who had also been lost in thought with a serious expression, approached with a smile.

blocks.speaker: Nane
blocks.content: Because I have legs. I can go anywhere, can't I?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Don't dodge the question.
Shirone spoke firmly.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If your heart doesn't move, your legs don't either. You've never wavered before, so why did you suddenly come here?

blocks.speaker: Nane
blocks.content: There's something… strange there.
Nane pointed toward the inner fortress of the northern front.

blocks.speaker: Nane
blocks.content: A being with universal constants different from this world. That also means… micro-adjustments have been applied.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Micro-adjustments?

blocks.speaker: Nane
blocks.content: There's a limit to the outcomes of events in the universe. For example, if you create a world where a small ball moves, you can't set the ball's speed, mass, and direction infinitely.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Because it would exceed the scope of creation.

blocks.speaker: Nane
blocks.content: Exactly. Therefore, if all characteristics were quantified, the limit of those values would match precisely with the limits of this world's settings. The being closest to that limit is Ymir.
Nane raised his index finger.

blocks.speaker: Nane
blocks.content: Of course, if you only look at power, supernova explosions or black holes are stronger. But they are slaves to the natural order and its laws. When you consider the overall sum, Ymir ranks higher. Free will, after all, is an overwhelmingly powerful attribute.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Even a mere child could set a mountain ablaze… is that what you mean?

blocks.speaker: Nane
blocks.content: Precisely. But the neo-humans exist outside that boundary. Whether it's physical, sensory, mental, or logical attributes, they are beings subjected to micro-adjustments. They won't be easy to stop.
Shirone also came to understand this through his battle with Bardvelve.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If it hadn't been for Nane, the battle would have been much tougher. The same goes for him. But still…

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: There was one question that remained unanswered. Why are you helping me? Closing this world was supposed to be your goal, wasn't it?

blocks.speaker: Nane
blocks.content: The situation has changed. If the world remains closed, I'd be the sole truth. But you've opened the ceiling. In this open state, the truth of this place loses its absoluteness. Even for me, a Buddha, that's an undesirable outcome.
Nane began walking toward the inner fortress.

blocks.speaker: Nane
blocks.content: Let's go. Before making any decisions, we need to confirm something. You should see it with your own eyes as well.
The horrific truth.
When Borbor opened her eyes, she found herself hanging with both hands tied by ropes to the ceiling.
As she blinked, her vision filled with the grotesque faces of dozens of insect-like grasshopper men.

blocks.speaker: Borbor
blocks.content: Ki-iii… an enemy of our kin.
Though they carried no special weapons, their prominent jaws and claws were threatening enough.
Borbor's lips twisted in a bitter smile.

blocks.speaker: Borbor
blocks.content: I'm still alive.

blocks.speaker: Borbor
blocks.content: If life was suffering and death was fear, what could you call being dragged back to life after dying?

blocks.speaker: Borbor
blocks.content: One of the grasshopper men approached her.

blocks.speaker: Grasshopper Man
blocks.content: Don't expect a quick death.
This one was larger than the others, likely their leader. His grotesque jaw jutted forward menacingly.

blocks.speaker: Grasshopper Man
blocks.content: I'll tear you apart piece by piece. Watch as your flesh gets devoured by our throats, you pathetic livestock.

blocks.speaker: Borbor
blocks.content: Again… All human deaths are destined to be lonely deaths. So, I'm supposed to keep living.

blocks.speaker: Borbor
blocks.content: A lonely existence. No matter how desperately you live or how quickly you vanish, nothing changes. Life feels like endlessly shoveling into the void.

blocks.speaker: Borbor
blocks.content: It's not that living is sweet. It's just that dying is infuriating. There's no honey-coated world out there.

blocks.speaker: Borbor
blocks.content: Bark! Go on, bark!
When the grasshopper man drove his claw into her side, sparks danced in Borbor's vision.
Yet her expression didn't change.

blocks.speaker: Grasshopper Man
blocks.content: Heh. You're holding up well. Then how about this?
As her flesh split and her bones scraped against something, a scream erupted from Borbor's mouth.
The grasshopper men burst into laughter.

blocks.speaker: Grasshopper Man
blocks.content: Good! That's the sound we wanted!
Fueled by his comrades' laughter, the leader drove his claws even deeper.
The leader stopped mid-sentence when he saw Borbor's eyes, still cold and unflinching.

blocks.speaker: Borbor
blocks.content: What the…? In her pupils, the grotesque face of the insect leader reflected clearly, without a hint of emotion.
As the leader tilted his head slightly, Borbor mirrored the motion at the exact same angle.

blocks.speaker: Borbor
blocks.content: No, that's not right. The leader was confused.

blocks.speaker: Borbor
blocks.content: Why do I feel like I'm copying this livestock?

blocks.speaker: Borbor
blocks.content: Gouge out my eyes.
For a moment, the leader couldn't tell whether the voice he had heard was Borbor's or his own.

blocks.speaker: Grasshopper Man
blocks.content: Y-Yeah!
Standing at the boundary where self and other blurred, he reached toward Borbor's eyes with both hands.
I'll gouge them out!

blocks.speaker: Borbor
blocks.content: Huh? H-Hey?
The other grasshopper men recoiled in shock.

blocks.speaker: Grasshopper Man
blocks.content: Hey! Wait a second! What are you doing?
Even as they shouted, the leader grabbed his own eye with his clawed hands.
Arrgh! Die, filthy livestock!

blocks.speaker: Borbor
blocks.content: Arrgh! Die, filthy livestock!
With a sickening pop, the leader's eye burst, spilling thick, viscous fluid onto his hands.
He staggered back, clutching the mangled remains of his eye.

blocks.speaker: Grasshopper Man
blocks.content: Hah… hah… I did it! I got my revenge!

blocks.speaker: Grasshopper Man
blocks.content: What the hell…?
While the other grasshopper men stood frozen in disbelief, Borbor slowly turned her head.

blocks.speaker: Borbor
blocks.content: Next.
As Shirone and Nane opened the door, they were met by a young girl with a simple, innocent appearance.
However, the scene quickly turned unsettling—the girl's eyes were pitch black, gleaming with small specks of light, and she was gnawing on a severed arm.
Shirone felt a chill run down his spine.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I can't tell what she is.

blocks.speaker: Nane
blocks.content: She's certainly peculiar.
Nane remarked with a grin as he approached her.

blocks.speaker: Nane
blocks.content: No matter how the laws of the universe bend, something like this shouldn't exist. You, being beyond truth, what are you?

blocks.speaker: Girl
blocks.content: God.
No further proof was necessary.

blocks.speaker: Girl
blocks.content: Since the moment I came into existence, everything has been beneath me. Now, hand over your arms and legs.
The moment her words ended, an unprecedented killing intent filled the room.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: A disaster… The reason Yahweh and Buddha could divide philosophy between them was because they operated within a closed world, a world clearly defined by humanity. Philosophy is for humans, defined by humans. Beings beyond that boundary shouldn't have the right to define it.
Nane drew his sword of sermon and approached.

blocks.speaker: Nane
blocks.content: Let's see what you've got.
Shirone shattered the Hexa and summoned his Hand of God, but the girl remained unfazed.

blocks.speaker: Girl
blocks.content: Hmm, you want to fight me?

blocks.speaker: Nane
blocks.content: Fight? I wonder if there's even a point where you and we can truly clash.

blocks.speaker: Girl
blocks.content: True…
After gnawing off the last bit of flesh, she tossed aside the bone and stood up.

blocks.speaker: Girl
blocks.content: Come at me.
As her lips twisted into a grotesque smile, Shirone and Nane lunged forward simultaneously.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Please… Gritting his teeth, Shirone swung his arm, Hand of God mounted over his shoulder.
Let something remain.
Amid the silence that transcended the roar of explosions, the northern city ignited in blinding flashes of light.
Boom! Boom!
At an altitude of 500 meters, Asraiker and Gaitan watched the battle unfold below.
This was not a fight they could interfere in.
Holding the unconscious Borbor by the wrist, Gaitan spoke with a grave expression.

blocks.speaker: Gaitan
blocks.content: She has unusual eyes.

blocks.speaker: Asraiker
blocks.content: Yes. It seems like she's interpreting photon signals and converting them into quantum signals, ignoring causality entirely.
A mountain was cleanly sliced in half.
It seemed the girl was imagining an enormous pair of scissors, shredding the landscape at will.

blocks.speaker: Asraiker
blocks.content: Her ability is like a combination of Yahweh's subjectivity and Buddha's objectivity. But even so…

blocks.speaker: Asraiker
blocks.content: Despite her power, the girl was the one being overwhelmed.

blocks.speaker: Asraiker
blocks.content: Their coordination is astonishing. If there were even a slight mental gap between them, this battle would already be over. It's ironic—no one understands Yahweh better than Buddha.
Asraiker's eyes glowed as a magic circle formed within them, magnifying the scene on the ground.

blocks.speaker: Asraiker
blocks.content: …It's over.

blocks.speaker: Girl
blocks.content: Hah… hah…
The girl, Piri, was in a wretched state—her left eye was gone, both legs severed below the knees, and her right arm grotesquely bent and broken.

blocks.speaker: Girl
blocks.content: How… how did you…

blocks.speaker: Asraiker
blocks.content: Given her ability to defy this world's signals, it was abnormal for her to suffer any injuries at all.

blocks.speaker: Asraiker
blocks.content: You can speak, can't you?

blocks.speaker: Girl
blocks.content: Did you understand that?
To Piri, 99.99% of the language spoken by humanity sounded like the barking of beasts.

blocks.speaker: Girl
blocks.content: Well, that's an honor.
A massive sword formed behind Nane, while Shirone unleashed a miraculous stream of energy.
As the Hand of God grasped Nane's sword of sermon, it pointed directly at Piri from the heavens.

blocks.speaker: Nane
blocks.content: N-No…

blocks.speaker: Girl
blocks.content: You're nothing but livestock! You can't harm me, the leader of the Neo-Humans! This is all a lie!

blocks.speaker: Nane
blocks.content: Sermon.

blocks.speaker: Nane
blocks.content: Heart.
Shirone invoked.
A red flash descended from the sky, slicing through the world and transcending time itself.
Gah!
Piri's death throes ended abruptly as her severed head rolled across the ground.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Everything… is a lie.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Even in her final moments, she couldn't acknowledge her defeat.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: She was strong… If another being like her appears, we won't be able to handle it.

blocks.speaker: Nane
blocks.content: This was just a prelude, Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Nane
blocks.content: A prelude?

blocks.speaker: Nane
blocks.content: If the goal was simply to defeat you, they could have created an unimaginable monster. But doing so would require resetting everything in the universe from its initial stages. Destroying you while keeping this world intact is the second filter. If they push one step further, they'll annihilate all of humanity.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Perhaps it was because it was Nane speaking that Shirone finally realized the source of his unease.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What are you planning? Why did you come to the northern front? You said you wanted to confirm something.

blocks.speaker: Nane
blocks.content: The world has opened. Because of that, I need to find the answer to one question. Answer me, Shirone. Am I Nane, who swallowed Anke Ra's dream…?

blocks.speaker: Nane
blocks.content: Or am I Anke Ra's dream, who swallowed Nane?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: In a closed world, it didn't matter. But now, it's the most crucial question.

blocks.speaker: Nane
blocks.content: I will go to the outer world. If I still remain human there…

blocks.speaker: Nane
blocks.content: Then perhaps I too can become a god with a heart. I will eliminate the suffering of all beings.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Nane…

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Perhaps, beyond this world, the two of them sought the same thing.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But…

blocks.speaker: Nane
blocks.content: The smile vanished.

blocks.speaker: Nane
blocks.content: If I am merely a program, the human known as Nane will cease to exist. When that happens, I leave the rest to you. And…

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I may have opened the world, but Nane could have closed it again. He could have… if he had chosen to kill Amy.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But in the end, he chose his heart, ready to face eternal annihilation.

blocks.speaker: Nane
blocks.content: Let me go instead. This is the world I want to protect, so it's only right that I be the one to go.

blocks.speaker: Nane
blocks.content: Only I, who swallowed Anke Ra's dream, can pass through the filter. If you leave the photon realm, humanity will perish. We can't waste this one final chance.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But…

blocks.speaker: Nane
blocks.content: I opened the spirit realm. This pain is my burden, so allow me to end it.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Who cares about who goes first now…

blocks.speaker: Nane
blocks.content: Please.
As Nane turned away, his body shimmered and countless swords of doctrine shot outward.
Graaah!
As the sound of the swords flying to the ends of the universe echoed back, Shirone asked.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Won't you regret it?

blocks.speaker: Nane
blocks.content: Nane closed his eyes and smiled faintly.

blocks.speaker: Nane
blocks.content: I will.

blocks.speaker: Nane
blocks.content: Until the very end, one face remained etched in his mind.

blocks.speaker: Nane
blocks.content: Regret… He was leaving with a heart burdened by longing.
As Nane opened his eyes, the swords of doctrine compressed space and pierced his body.
Boom!
A blinding light burst from Nane's eyes, turning the entire world into a blank sheet.

blocks.speaker: Nane
blocks.content: Now, let's see what lies ahead.
When the landscape regained its colors, Nane had vanished without a trace, leaving not even a sound behind.
